description: Close the remaining quality gaps after baseline stabilization by completing compatibility assertions, eliminating runtime warning noise, aligning stat dictionaries, and introducing first-stage bundle control with measurable outcomes.

userStories.title: Make Compatibility Tests Executable
userStories.description: As a developer, I want legacy compatibility checks to be real assertions so that compatibility regressions are detectable in the default gate.
acceptanceCriteria: AllTests no longer prints compatibility tests pending implementation
Legacy input/output compatibility assertions are executable

userStories.title: Unify Stat Dictionary and Validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want stat definitions and runtime validation to be consistent so that unknown stat warnings do not occur in normal test runs.
acceptanceCriteria: No Unknown stat: charisma warning appears in the real test gate
Stat key definitions are aligned across types, data, and executors

userStories.title: Clean Test Environment Warnings
userStories.description: As a team, I want test environment initialization to be clean so that warning noise does not mask real issues.
acceptanceCriteria: No localstorage-file invalid path warning appears during test gate execution
Local and CI environment setup paths are defined and valid

userStories.title: First-Stage Bundle Size Control
userStories.description: As a frontend engineer, I want to reduce main bundle weight through low-risk splitting so that delivery performance does not degrade as features grow.
acceptanceCriteria: Main bundle size is measurably reduced from the current baseline (~486KB)

userStories.title: Document Release Quality Contract
userStories.description: As a team, I want a documented release validation contract including warning hygiene so that pre-merge quality checks are explicit and enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation defines required checks: typecheck + build + real test gate + key warning-free logs
Validation workflow is reproducible for local and CI
No unresolved critical warnings remain in release checklist
